mission.title: kcp: feat: add a workspace tree subcommand
mission.description: feat: add a workspace tree subcommand. This issue affects 3+ users.

steps.title: Identify kcp feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kcp deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=kcp
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: feat: add a workspace tree subcommand

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kcp resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
### Feature Description

It would be nice to have a workspace tree subcommand to visualize the hierarchy of workspaces, i.e.

```
NAME           TYPE           PHASE   URL
------------- -------------

steps.title: Apply the fix for feat: add a workspace tree subcommand
steps.description:
## Summary

Workspaces being nested objects its quite hard to keep mental image of the system in your head. 
Simple command to show workspace tree. Added -f to show full paths too so it is easier to use with `workspace use full_path`
```
[mjudeikis@unknown kcp]$ go run ./cmd/kubectl-workspaces/ tree
```yaml
NAME           TYPE           PHASE   URL
------------- ------------- -------- -----
root           universal      Ready   ...
 ├─ org1       organization   Ready   ...
 │   ├─ team1  organization   Ready   ...
 │   └─ team2  organization   Ready   ...
 └─ foo        user           Ready   ...
```

steps.title: Confirm feat: add a workspace tree subcommand is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: ## Summary

Workspaces being nested objects its quite hard to keep mental image of the system in your head. 
Simple command to show workspace tree. Added -f to show full paths too so it is easier to use with `workspace use full_path`
```
[mjudeikis@unknown kcp]$ go run ./cmd/kubectl-workspaces/ tree -f                                                                                                 . This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
NAME           TYPE           PHASE   URL
------------- ------------- -------- -----
root           universal      Ready   ...
 ├─ org1       organization   Ready   ...
 │   ├─ team1  organization   Ready   ...
 │   └─ team2  organization   Ready   ...
 └─ foo        user           Ready   ...

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kcp installed or the issue environment reproducible.
